preview_text:
The model estimates Morocco as a clear favorite with a 74% win probability, compared to Haiti's 5% chance. This represents a substantial gap in expected performance across the two sides at a neutral venue.

The 21% draw probability suggests meaningful competitive distance—Morocco is favored but not overwhelmingly so in a single match context. The W/D/L spread (74/21/5) indicates the model sees Haiti as unlikely to win outright, though a stalemate carries genuine plausibility given the wide margin between the top two outcomes.

The market consensus sits notably higher on Morocco, at 80%, while compressing the draw to 14%. This 6-percentage-point gap between model and sportsbook on Morocco's win probability reflects meaningful disagreement; the market views Haiti's upset chance as slightly lower and a draw as less probable than the model suggests. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment proves correct.
